commands.repair_reason: Human review should accept the compatibility boundary and reconcile the overlapping history PR; there is no narrow automated repair from this pass.; Cleared: The diff does not add dependencies, workflows, install scripts, package metadata, or credential handling, and it includes a sensitive-media guard for durable transcript mirrors. commands.review_summary:
The PR routes message-tool-only internal source replies back into TUI/WebChat-style Gateway chat events and history while preserving the legacy `webchat` tool-result channel.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level review: current main's route resolver only blocks WebChat clients and the current tests still expect `openclaw-tui` UI clients on channel-scoped sessions to inherit an external route. The PR body also includes an exact-head live TUI/Gateway proof and a base negative control.
commands.review_followup: Human review should accept the compatibility boundary and reconcile the overlapping history PR; there is no narrow automated repair from this pass.

target.title: fix(gateway): deliver internal source replies to TUI
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix message-tool source replies from internal OpenClaw chat surfaces so TUI/WebChat receive the visible reply in the active run instead of inheriting a stale external delivery route.
- Keep the legacy tool-result/action channel as `webchat` for compatibility, and use `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"` as the internal sink marker.
- Keep external channels such as Telegram, Slack, and Discord as separate deliverable channels; this does not make their conversations visible in WebChat/TUI.
- Split the source-reply display/mirror path into focused gateway helpers, add coverage for current-run routing, and remove PR-introduced dead exports (`isTuiClient`, `GatewayCurrentSessionClientId`, `GatewayInternalChatSurfaceClientId`).

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #38957

## Design Boundary

This PR intentionally does not create a new public channel, config key, CLI flag, provider policy, or external delivery path. `internal-ui` is an internal sink marker for message-tool source replies on OpenClaw-owned chat surfaces such as TUI, WebChat, Control UI, and native UI mode.

The compatibility boundary is that `MessageActionRunResult.channel` and the model-facing result payload `channel` stay `webchat`. Existing agents, workflows, downstream parsers, and source copies that key on the historical message-tool `channel` value continue to see the old route label. New code should read `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"` when it needs to know that the visible source reply was delivered into the OpenClaw-owned internal UI sink.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: TUI-originated `message_tool_only` source replies render in the active TUI/current-run chat even when the session has a stale external delivery route, and the tool result keeps `channel: "webchat"` while identifying the visible sink with `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"`.

Real environment tested: Live local dev host on exact PR head `35f9d69e5376d8e91200aa5bb2dec7d05eaa471e` (`OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (35f9d69)`), isolated staged OpenClaw home at `/tmp/openclaw-test-home-crK8kR`, foreground Gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`, throwaway loopback token auth, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, `messages.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`, `gateway.controlUi.enabled: false`, bundled plugins from this worktree, live `openai/gpt-5.5` / `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, seeded session route `lastChannel: "telegram"` and `lastTo: "6101296751"` before the TUI-mode run. A full terminal TUI PTY proof was also run on the previous behavior head `088b030cf073f8731b74354b202faf0c17792f57`; the latest head only adds named adapter constants around the same sink/target values.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
PROOF_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-test-home-crK8kR
SESSION=agent:main:tui-live-proof-terminal-202605201207

HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \
USERPROFILE="$PROOF_HOME" \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST_QUIET=1 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18789 \
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run \
  --port 18789 \
  --bind loopback \
  --auth token \
  --token '<throwaway proof token>' \
  --allow-unconfigured \
  --ws-log compact

HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \
USERPROFILE="$PROOF_HOME" \
node scripts/run-node.mjs tui \
  --url ws://127.0.0.1:18789 \
  --token '<throwaway proof token>' \
  --session "$SESSION" \
  --timeout-ms 180000
```

In the real terminal TUI PTY, I typed and submitted:

```text
Live routing proof from the real terminal TUI. You must use the message tool, not ordinary final text. Send exactly this text to the current TUI chat run: TUI TERMINAL LIVE PROOF TUI-TERM-1779278877-26056. No extra words, no markdown, no quotes.
```

On exact PR head `35f9d69e5376d8e91200aa5bb2dec7d05eaa471e`, I ran a TUI-mode WebSocket proof client (`client.id: "openclaw-tui"`, `mode: "ui"`) against a separate seeded stale-route session, sent the same message-tool-only prompt through `chat.send`, waited for the live `chat` final event, and then queried `chat.history` over the Gateway WebSocket.

Evidence after fix: The actual terminal TUI rendered this standalone assistant reply after the typed prompt:

```text
TUI TERMINAL LIVE PROOF TUI-TERM-1779278877-26056
```

The `chat.history` verifier for `agent:main:tui-live-proof-terminal-202605201207` returned:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:tui-live-proof-terminal-202605201207",
  "historyContainsExpected": true,
  "messageCount": 4,
  "hasSourceReplySink": true,
  "hasCurrentRunTarget": true,
  "expected": "TUI TERMINAL LIVE PROOF TUI-TERM-1779278877-26056"
}
```

The separate TUI-mode WebSocket proof used stale route `{ "lastChannel": "telegram", "lastTo": "6101296751" }`, skipped channels, and received a live chat final with run id `idem-tui-live-1779278321-8006:internal-source-reply:0`; its `chat.history` check returned `historyContainsExpected: true`.

The exact-head TUI-mode WebSocket proof after the adapter cleanup returned:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "head": "35f9d69e5376d8e91200aa5bb2dec7d05eaa471e",
  "client": {
    "id": "openclaw-tui",
    "mode": "ui"
  },
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:tui-pattern-proof-1779280517818",
  "runId": "idem-tui-pattern-1779280517818",
  "staleRouteBefore": {
    "lastChannel": "telegram",
    "lastTo": "6101296751",
    "deliveryContext": {
      "channel": "telegram",
      "to": "6101296751"
    }
  },
  "liveFinalRunId": "idem-tui-pattern-1779280517818:internal-source-reply:0",
  "liveFinalText": "TUI PATTERN EXACT HEAD PROOF idem-tui-pattern-1779280517818",
  "historyContainsExpected": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The message-tool source reply was visible in the active terminal TUI on the full PTY run, visible as a live `chat` final to the TUI-mode Gateway client on exact head `35f9d69e5376d8e91200aa5bb2dec7d05eaa471e`, persisted into `chat.history`, and resolved to the current internal UI run despite the stale Telegram route and skipped external channels. The proof path would have had no working Telegram delivery fallback because channels were intentionally skipped.

What was not tested: Live Telegram/Slack/Discord delivery was not rerun because this PR does not change their external delivery paths. The full terminal PTY proof was not repeated after the adapter-only constant extraction; exact-head proof used the same Gateway path with a TUI-mode client identity and verified the live `chat` final plus `chat.history`. Control UI static assets were disabled in the proof config to avoid an unrelated local `ui:build` startup delay. The throwaway proof token and temp proof home were not committed.

Before evidence: Earlier base negative control on exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834` reproduced the failure shape: the model used the `message` tool and the source reply was persisted with `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"`, but the active TUI returned to idle without rendering the standalone reply.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/utils/message-channel.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts` (6 files, 175 tests passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` after the adapter cleanup (5 files, 175 tests passed)
- `codex review --base origin/main` (clean; also ran `pnpm tsgo:prod` successfully during review)
- `codex review --base origin/main` after the adapter cleanup (clean; no actionable findings)
- Dead-code audit: `git grep -n "\bisTuiClient\b\|\bGatewayCurrentSessionClientId\b\|\bGatewayInternalChatSurfaceClientId\b" -- . ':!node_modules' ':!dist' ':!coverage' || true` returned no matches
- Exact-head live TUI-mode Gateway proof on `35f9d69e5376d8e91200aa5bb2dec7d05eaa471e`, described above

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: yes
- Config/env changes: no
- Public SDK/API changes: no
- External channel behavior changes: no
- Migration needed: no
